title: Project Telos current-state and revival boundary

claims.text: A production Telos research corpus must preserve source identity, rights posture, and provenance before full-text use.
claims.falsification: A production full-text lane accepts a source without item-level rights or provenance fields.

claims.text: Large-workspace context should be lossless by reference, not hidden payload or steganographic required context.
claims.falsification: A context envelope requires hidden payloads or lacks source refs and hashes.

claims.text: Every quality tool promoted into Telos must attach to at least one of the five flagships through a typed host boundary.
claims.falsification: A promoted tool has no flagship host or no CLI/MCP adapter roadmap.

claims.text: Agent action receipts must separate operational decision, verification verdict, and failure code.
claims.falsification: A denial, binding failure, and unverifiable verifier result collapse into one guardrail outcome.

claims.text: Creative and rendering engine outputs must preserve source, transform, measurement, render plan or backend, and receipt hashes.
claims.falsification: An export contains only pixels or screenshots with no receipt or measurement summary.
